channel.title: Memory-Systems on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Memory-Systems on emsenn.net

item.title: OpenClaw Continuous Learning and Behavioral Evolution
item.description:
Overview

OpenClaw achieves continuous learning and behavioral evolution through a three-layer architecture that persists knowledge across sessions, enables agents to autonomously extend capabilities, and filters learning opportunities through managed input gating. Unlike stateless chatbots that reset after each conversation, OpenClaw agents accumulate durable context, self-modify through skill creation, and evolve their decision boundaries through policy mechanisms.

Memory Persistence Layer

OpenClaw treats memory as the primary mechanism for accumulation across sessions. The memory system is Markdown-based and file-first, making it auditable and portable.

item.title: OpenClaw Memory
item.description:
OpenClaw memory is the Markdown-based memory layer in an OpenClaw workspace. Its source of truth is written files on disk rather than session-local model state.

Structure

The official memory docs describe two default durable layers:

  • MEMORY.md for curated long-term memory
  • memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md for dated logs and running context

Memory search tools are provided by the active memory plugin, but the files remain primary. Search augments retrieval; it does not replace the underlying Markdown record.

item.title: QMD
item.description:
QMD is a local-first search sidecar that OpenClaw can use as an experimental memory backend. In this mode, Markdown remains the source of truth while OpenClaw shells out to QMD for retrieval.

Role

The official memory docs describe QMD as combining BM25, vectors, and reranking over local Markdown-backed memory. OpenClaw can opt into it with memory.backend = "qmd".

Scope

QMD is not OpenClaw’s canonical memory store. It is a retrieval layer that sits on top of files such as MEMORY.md and memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md to improve search and recall.
